title: Virgin Galactic (NYSE: SPCE) Stock Outlook: Q3 2025 Results, Cash Burn, Analyst Targets and the 2026 Spaceflight Countdown
text: Virgin Galactic reported Q3 2025 results that continue the familiar pattern: very small revenue, large operating losses, and heavy cash burn. Revenue was just $365,000, with Q3 net loss roughly $64-64.4 million, an improvement from the $75 million loss in the prior year quarter as operating expenses declined. Free cash flow improved to negative $108 million, with $424 million in cash remaining at quarter-end. However, convertible notes of $421 million are due in 2027, creating a critical refinancing challenge. Cash resources are being sustained by equity issuance under the ATM program, with the company's cash declining from approximately $567 million in Q1 2025 to $424 million by Q3 2025. Management reaffirmed plans for flight testing to commence in Q3 2026 and commercial service beginning in Q4 2026. With two operational spaceships, Virgin Galactic projects approximately $450 million in annual revenue and $100 million in adjusted EBITDA at steady-state operations. The Q3 2025 earnings highlight both progress toward operational readiness and intensifying financial pressure.
